ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A 3D Visualizer creates 3D models and visualizations to communicate ideas and concepts in various fields such as architecture, product design, and video games.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: A 3D Visualizer uses computer-aided design (CAD) software and other tools to create 3D models, animations, and visualizations. They work closely with designers, architects, and other stakeholders to understand project requirements and deliver high-quality visualizations.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in art, design, and technology can pursue a career as a 3D Visualizer.
target_degrees: B.Des in Animation and Visual Effects

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in a related field can transition into a 3D Visualizer role by acquiring relevant skills and certifications.

future_builder.description: Experienced 3D Visualizers can advance their careers by learning new software, techniques, and tools to stay up-to-date with industry trends.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience as a 3D Visualizer

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into a 3D Visualizer role by acquiring relevant skills and building a portfolio.
